column1: # Curated TAB-separated list of upstream tests that the C# SDK deliberately
# does NOT port. The companion script `coverage-map.sh` reads this file when
# building `coverage-map.tsv` and accepts each row as an explicit N/A
# mapping. The `--check` gate rejects an empty reason column.
#   not-ported: <symbol>       — C# does not expose this upstream API.
#   e2e-only: live-backend     — upstream only exercises this under a live
#                                Turnkey backend; C# defers to Tier-4 opt-in.
# ---- proof verifier (proof-tests.ts) — all N/A, proof scope-out ----

column2: proof-fixture-only — proof verifier is outside the supported API; fixture structure and provenance are checked by ProofFixtureProvenanceTests
proof-fixture-only — paired app/boot proof data is retained for provenance, not cryptographic verification
proof-fixture-only — malformed app-proof verification requires the unported proof verifier
proof-fixture-only — malformed boot-proof verification requires the unported proof verifier

column1: # ---- async polling (async-test.ts) — all N/A, withAsyncPolling unported ----

column2: not-ported: withAsyncPolling — polling and activity lifecycle orchestration are outside the request-signing API
not-ported: withAsyncPolling — consensus-required polling errors belong to the unported full HTTP client
not-ported: withAsyncPolling — rejected-activity polling errors belong to the unported full HTTP client
not-ported: withAsyncPolling — failed-activity polling errors belong to the unported full HTTP client
not-ported: withAsyncPolling — synchronous activity polling is outside the request-signing API
not-ported: withAsyncPolling — typecheck-only test; not a runtime equivalence target
not-ported: hpkeAuthEncrypt — authenticated sender mode is outside the standard-mode HPKE API
not-ported: extractPrivateKeyFromPKCS8Bytes — PKCS#8 import is outside the supported bundle API
not-ported: verifyRequestStamp — the package constructs signed requests but does not expose a request-stamp verifier

column1: # ---- DER parser negative tests (crypto-test.ts:263-454) — fromDerSignature unported ----

column2: not-ported: fromDerSignature — DER input parsing is outside the supported Crypto API; stamper output DER is checked by ApiKeyStamperTests.SignWithApiKey_ProducesDerHexThatVerifies
not-ported: fromDerSignature — same as above; this case covers 33-byte INTEGER with leading zero
not-ported: fromDerSignature — missing SEQUENCE tag (0x30)
not-ported: fromDerSignature — empty signature negative path
not-ported: fromDerSignature — too-short signature negative path
not-ported: fromDerSignature — unsupported length encoding (0x80-0xFE)
not-ported: fromDerSignature — short-form length boundary case
not-ported: fromDerSignature — invalid r length
not-ported: fromDerSignature — invalid s length
not-ported: fromDerSignature — invalid non-padding r bytes
not-ported: fromDerSignature — invalid non-padding s bytes
not-ported: fromDerSignature — invalid r INTEGER tag
not-ported: fromDerSignature — invalid s INTEGER tag

column1: # The Turnkey fixture row is covered by the deterministic PureJS fixture. The
# OpenSSL keypair row exercises nodecrypto / WebCrypto / universal runtimes that
# this single-provider C# implementation does not expose.

column2: runtime-variant: OpenSSL-keypair stamping under nodecrypto / WebCrypto / universal is not ported; the supported deterministic PureJS-equivalent path is covered by ApiKeyStamperTests
e2e-only: live-backend — gRPC status-detail decoding requires the unported full HTTP client and a real backend error response
